context: Evaluates the `buildTree` function that converts a flat array of notebook objects with `parent_id` references into a nested `NotebookNode` tree. The implementation must correctly handle root nodes, single-level children, multi-level nesting, and maintain sort order among siblings. Efficient single-pass construction using a map lookup is expected.

checklist.name: NotebookNode interface
checklist.description: Defines or uses a `NotebookNode` type that extends `Notebook` with a `children: NotebookNode[]` array, enabling recursive nesting.

checklist.name: Root node identification
checklist.description: Correctly identifies root notebooks as those where `parent_id` is `null` or `undefined`, and places only these in the returned top-level array.

checklist.name: Child node placement
checklist.description: Places each notebook with a non-null `parent_id` into the `children` array of the corresponding parent node rather than in the root array.

checklist.name: Multi-level nesting
checklist.description: Correctly handles notebooks nested more than one level deep: a grandchild appears in its parent's `children`, not at the root level.

checklist.name: Map-based algorithm
checklist.description: Uses a map (object or `Map`) keyed by notebook ID for O(n) construction, avoiding nested array searches for parent lookup.
